interface: HEC REST endpoints under /services/collector with indexer acknowledgment

auth.scheme: Authorization: Splunk <token> header on every HEC request; the token never goes in a URL, a query string, or an error message

channel.rule: with indexer acknowledgment enabled every data send and every ack query must carry a channel ID; the channel must be a GUID (8-4-4-4-12 hex) but can be randomly generated
channel.pinned_usage: event and ack requests use the header; raw requests use the query parameter

send_events.body: batched envelopes: one compact JSON object per event, newline-separated. Envelope keys: event (required; string or JSON object) plus optional time/host/source/sourcetype/index/fields
send_events.time_format: UNIX epoch seconds with millisecond precision, e.g. 1770000000.250
send_events.fields_rule: fields is a flat JSON object of index-time custom fields; values may be scalars or arrays of scalars, never nested objects; envelopes carrying fields are only indexed by the /event endpoint
send_events.success: 200 with {"text":"Success","code":0,"ackId":<int>} when the token has indexer acknowledgment enabled; a success without ackId means acknowledgment is off for the token

send_raw.body: raw event bytes passed through unmodified

poll_acks.success: 200 with {"acks": {"<ackId>": true|false, ...}} — object keys are strings
poll_acks.true_means: the events behind that ackId were indexed and replicated; false means status unknown (not yet a failure)
poll_acks.delete_on_true: once HEC reports true for an ackId it deletes that status; querying the same ackId again always returns false, so clients must stop querying an ackId after the first true

health.responses: 200 {"text":"HEC is healthy","code":17}; 503 {"text":"HEC is unhealthy, queues are full","code":18}

gzip.rule: senders may gzip-compress a data POST body; the request must then carry Content-Encoding: gzip

18.text: HEC is unhealthy, queues are full

26.text: HEC queue is at capacity and cannot process any more requests

errors.retry_rule: retry transport failures, 429, 500 and 503 with injected backoff sleeps; never retry other 4xx responses

ambiguous_failure.rule: when a send dies without a readable response the batch state is unknown; because indexer acknowledgment provides delivery confirmation, the safe behavior is to resend the identical batch (at-least-once) and rely on ack polling for confirmation
